title: Project Manager Digital Health (m/w/d)
location: Germany (Koblenz or other CGM locations; occasional travel to clients)
required_skills: Prior experience as Project Manager in IT environment
Experience managing standard software projects in medical/healthcare context
Project management certification (PRINCE2, IPMA, PMI) or comparable practical leadership experience
Knowledge of medical communication standards (HL7, DICOM)
General willingness to travel for client visits
bonus_skills: Fast comprehension of complex project scenarios
matched_skills: Project coordination and management experience (Hapag-Lloyd, HSBC — multi-workstream delivery)
Project documentation, budget tracking, project status management (HSBC)
Coordinating internal and external project teams (HSBC — 6 workstreams; Hapag-Lloyd)
missing_skills: No healthcare or medical software project management experience
No knowledge of HL7 or DICOM medical communication standards
No IT project management in software deployment context
Very good German required — candidate has B1 (likely insufficient for client-facing German healthcare sector role)
No experience with HCM or PACS systems
core_responsibilities: Responsible for successful delivery of HCM and PACS projects for customers
Independent planning and organisation of projects after onboarding
Coordination of internal and external project teams during execution
Monitor project status, derive corrective measures, maintain project documentation
Budget and effort management, project controlling and billing in coordination with client/sales/backoffice
recommended_emphasis: Do not apply — medical/healthcare IT context is domain-critical and requires HL7/DICOM knowledge
German proficiency at B1 is insufficient for client-facing project management in the German healthcare sector
Lack of PM certification is an additional gap
The general project coordination experience is relevant in principle but insufficient to overcome the domain-specific technical gaps
summary: Match score is 22.9 (poor_match). The candidate has genuine project coordination and budget tracking experience applicable to IT project management generally, but lacks the specific requirements: medical software context, HL7/DICOM knowledge, PM certification, and adequate German proficiency for client-facing healthcare projects. The domain barrier is high and the language requirement further limits viability. Do not apply.
